title: Unlimited Skills MCP distribution fixture E2E report
description: Machine report of scripts/run-mcp-profile-distribution-fixture-e2e.py (E26): the 23-step fixture-only workflow proving the planned registered/team distribution flow (E23 channels/assignments + the E24 registry carrier contract MODELED as fixture functions) end-to-end over the REAL client stack (E14/E15/E16/E17/E20, the gateway resolution, the E11 inspector). Per step: name, ok, key facts, the E25 abuse-case test ids (ABT-NNx) the step covers or models, duration. Fixture mode only: a local directory stands in for the registry, ephemeral DEV Ed25519 keys, private temp directories, no hosted calls, no real entitlement service, no production keys, no network, no telemetry. The report never contains key material, signature values, full SHA-256 hashes (prefixes only), argument values, or local paths (basenames at most).

mode.description: The harness has exactly one mode: fixture (nothing hosted exists to call; the real library root, trust store, and audit log are never touched).

ed25519.description: Always true: the E19 publisher has no fallback signature scheme, so the harness refuses to run without real Ed25519 (the optional cryptography package).

clock.description: The run's pinned verification clock (the run start, UTC); the expiry abuse steps inject explicit offset clocks on top.

steps.description: The executed steps in workflow order. A selection (--step NAME) runs and reports the workflow prefix ending at NAME; a failing step ends the array early.

abt_coverage.description: Sorted union of every ABT id cited by the executed steps (docs/mcp-distribution-abuse-test-plan.md). Empty only when the run failed before any abuse step; the verifier script requires a non-empty list.

exit_code.description: 0 only when every selected step passed.

abt_id.description: An E25 abuse-case test id (ABT-NNx); every cited id must exist in docs/mcp-distribution-abuse-test-plan.md (test-enforced).

facts.description: Key facts of the step: names, SHA-256 PREFIXES, counts, refusal codes/reasons, statuses, basenames. Never key material, full hashes, argument values, or local paths. A failed step carries a single path-scrubbed 'error' string instead.

abt.description: The E25 abuse-case test ids this step covers (client/e2e-owned) or models as fixture behavior (registry-owned).
